summary: Go ahead and make those changes

prompt.text: Go ahead and make those changes

response.text:
Changes are in and aligned to your latest requirements: Ctrl+C ends all active sessions, status defaults to accept as‑is, squash is lowercase `s`, OpenCode timeout and `/exit` handling added, and the session registry is now multi‑session with persistence.

What I updated
- End all sessions on Ctrl+C and persist registry to `~/.gg/daemon.json`, so daemon restarts can resume state (`src/daemon.rs`).
- `gg status` now accepts as‑is by default; `s` squashes, `a` amends, `u` undoes; squash/amend are constrained to latest commit and contiguous selection that includes HEAD (`src/status.rs`).
- Soft‑end support for OpenCode with a 600s timeout and `/exit` explicit end; end events are revocable when new activity arrives (`src/opencode.rs`, `schemas/opencode.md`).
- Cursor explicit end events now include `meta.end = true` (`src/cursor.rs`).
- Push is standard `git push`, no force‑with‑lease (`src/status.rs`, `src/git.rs`).
- `gg status` end detection now considers `soft_end` metadata (`src/git.rs`).
- Help text + README updated to match the new CLI and Ctrl+C behavior (`src/cli.rs`, `README.md`).

Files touched
- `src/daemon.rs`
- `src/status.rs`
- `src/opencode.rs`
- `src/cursor.rs`
- `src/git.rs`
- `src/cli.rs`
- `schemas/opencode.md`
- `README.md`

Notes
- Session end revocation works automatically: a new event after a soft/explicit end flips the session back to active because the last event is no longer an end marker.
- Ctrl+C now ends all active sessions and appends end events for each, but does not kill the daemon.

If you want me to validate quickly, I can run:
1) `cargo check`
2) `cargo test` (if you have tests)

Otherwise, tell me if you want any changes to the registry persistence path or the amend/squash constraints.

meta.session_title: Plan for a Git-based autonomous CLI daemon with commit automation
